member.name: Charlie Dewhirst
member.role: MP for Bridlington and The Wolds
member.synopsis: Charlie Dewhirst is the Conservative MP for Bridlington and The Wolds , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs: Regulation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, with reference to the Regulatory Policy Committee document, "Post-implementation reviews outstanding across government 2015 to 2026", updated 12 May 2026, what steps is the department
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Regulatory Policy Committee (RPC) identified one outstanding Post Implementation Review for Defra: The Reduction and Prevention of Agricultural Diffuse Pollution (England) Regulations 2018, commonly known as Farming Rules for Water. The Department has...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Health Services: Gender
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what guidance has (a) his Department, (b) NHS England and (c) MHPRA provides to NHS bodies and his Department’s arm’s length bodies on (a) the expression of gender critical views and (b) recognitio
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department of Health and Social Care has indicated that it will not be possible to answer this question within the usual time period. An answer is being prepared and will be provided as soon as it is available.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Department for Culture, Media and Sport: Marketing
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what information her Department holds on the cost it has spent on branded goods and merchandise.
writtenQuestions.answerText: DCMS spent £2,970 on 1,500 VE Day 80 branded lanyards in 2025 to support the safe delivery of the commemorative events. Lanyards help identify authorised personnel and manage access within a secure event footprint.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero: Marketing
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, how much his Department has spent on branded goods and merchandise since 24 April 2025; on what items; and at what cost.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The information can only be obtained at disproportionate cost.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero: Government Communication Service
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, how many headcount staff in his Department are (a) members of the Government Communication Network and (b) embedded communicators but not members of the Government Communication Network.
writtenQuestions.answerText: All embedded communicators are members of the Government Communication Service.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Department for Business and Trade: Government Communication Service
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, with reference to the Answer of 5 June 2026 to Question 3763 on Government Communications Service: Staff, and of 19 January 2026 to Question 104195 on Government Communication Service: Staff, how many
writtenQuestions.answerText: All embedded communicators are members of the Government Communication Service.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business and Trade

writtenQuestions.heading: Water Companies: Regulation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, with reference to page 17 of the Water White Paper of January 2026, whether the integrated water regulator will have the same budget as the Environment Agency, Natural England, Ofwat a
writtenQuestions.answerText: We are considering the detail of the funding arrangements that will be needed. In doing so, we will ensure that the new regulator will have the funds to deliver its responsibilities in full, balancing the interests of customers, investors and the environm...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Space Weather: National Security
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what assessment she has made of the recommendations in the National Audit Office report entitled The UK’s resilience to severe space weather, HC1737, March 2026.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government welcomes the National Audit Office’s report, The UK’s resilience to severe space weather, and recognises the value of its recommendations in strengthening the United Kingdom’s resilience to this national risk. The report identified progress...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Research: Disclosure of Information
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, whether the Government Office for Science provides guidance to Departments on the requirements to publish Area of Research Interest.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government Office for Science (GO-Science) provides guidance to departments on the publication of Areas of Research Interest (ARIs). GO-Science published updated guidance on GOV.UK in November 2024, which advises ministerial departments, non-ministeri...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: EU Files Forum
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, with reference to the Answer of 13 March 2026 to Question 117997 on EU Files Forum, if he will list the organisations that attended each of those meetings.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We would not release names of participants without their agreement.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business and Trade

writtenQuestions.heading: Wind Power
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what number and proportion of wind farms are owned or managed by organisations which are ultimately owned by Chinese companies.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The vast majority of UK wind farms have no direct ownership from China. We work very closely with industry and across government to maintain a detailed picture of foreign involvement in energy infrastructure. We continue to take a consistent, long-term an...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Universities: Admissions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, whether there is any guidance from (a) her Department and (b) the Office for Students on the use of POLAR3 and POLAR4 for university admissions by higher education providers.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department does not issue guidance to higher education (HE) providers on the use of Participation of Local Areas (POLAR)3 or POLAR4 in admissions decisions. Universities are autonomous institutions and are responsible for their own admissions policies...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Ofwat: Equality
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, pursuant to the answer of 22 June 2026, to Question 9341, on Ofwat: Equality, if she will provide a breakdown of the £4,200 expenditure of external EDI expenditure in 2025-26, includin
writtenQuestions.answerText: Below is the information sent to the Cabinet Office on EDI expenditure for 2025/26. This was sent to the Cabinet Office on 9 April. Notification of External EDI ExpenditureDepartmentOfwatDepartmental Business UnitOfwatAmount of expenditure£4,200Date expe...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Food: Labelling
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, with reference to the answer of 21 April 2026, to Question 125703, on Food: Labelling, whether the SPS agreement will lead to foods other than fruit jams, jellies & marmalades being re
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Sanitary and Phytosanitary (SPS) Agreement is still being negotiated and the final list of legislation in scope remains subject to negotiations. This Government has begun publishing sector specific information to help businesses get ready. As set out ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Department of Health and Social Care: Facilities Agreements
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether (a) his Department and (b) NHS England plan to issue revised guidance on trade union facility time in (i) the NHS and (ii) Arm’s Length Bodies.
writtenQuestions.answerText: There are currently no plans for the Department or NHS England to issue revised guidance on trade union facility time. Existing relevant legislative provisions are outlined in the ACAS Code of Practice on time off for trade union duties and activities and...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Department of Health and Social Care: Freud Communications
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what expenditure (a) his Department and (b) NHS England has undertaken via Freuds since July 2024, including via subcontractors.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department holds a contract with AMV, previously MullenLowe, which was procured following a competition under the CCS/GCA RM6125 Campaign Solutions Framework. The contract provides access to various subcontractors, including Freuds. Spend with AMV is ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs: Secondment
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, which organisations have (a) had departmental Civil Servants seconded to them and (b) seconded organisational staff to her Department since July 2024.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Since July 2024, a total of 45 organisations had Civil Servants seconded to them from Defra whilst in the same period, Defra received secondees from a total of 22 organisations.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs: Social Media
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, how much his Department has spent on social influencers directly and via contractors since 4 July 2024.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs has not spent any money on social influencers directly or via contractors since 4 July 2024.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Angling: Licensing
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what savings her Department has made to communications and marketing spending by cancelling the Environment Agency Fishing Licence Marketing Campaign since July 2024.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Following a Government wide review of campaigns with over 100k spend, it was communicated in September 2024 that the EA fishing licence campaign would not continue in 25/26. At this point the campaign's marketing budget for 24/25 (300k) had already been u...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs
